intro: Linear and Release Please diverge first on pricing model: Linear uses freemium pricing with a free tier, Release Please uses free pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. Linear is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and is not a fit for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement. Release Please is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and is not a fit for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement. On the scoring side, Linear rates 5/5 for developer experience and 4/5 for transparency; Release Please rates 5/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with Linear you accept that contact-based pricing scales steeply with list growth, while Release Please comes with the reality that contact-based pricing scales steeply with list growth. Neither side is free. Linear's headline strength in the data is that centralizes customer data and automates lifecycle messaging.
quick_take: Linear is for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys; Release Please is for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Linear if you are saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose Release Please if you are saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and a free pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.q: Is Linear cheaper than Release Please?
faq.a: Linear uses a freemium model, while Release Please uses a free model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Linear to Release Please?
faq.a: Linear has low lock-in (issue tracker, data exportable); Release Please has low lock-in (google oss github action). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in Linear; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.
Both Linear and Release Please score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so DX isn't the tiebreaker here. Decide on fit, pricing, or lock-in instead.

faq.q: Is Release Please a good alternative to Linear?
faq.a: Release Please is built for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys and explicitly not for single-product apps with no ongoing user engagement, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using Linear for saas and e-commerce teams managing user journeys, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
